channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: White House doctor says Trump is 'fully fit' in medical report
item.description: President Trump lost 20 pounds, lowered “bad” cholesterol and has “diverticulosis and a benign polyp,” according to a medical report released by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Video of Trump allegedly 'snubbing' actress Cheryl Hines spreads like wildfire but doesn't show full picture
item.description: President Donald Trump circled back to greet Cheryl Hines despite recent headlines claiming the president snubbed Secretary Kennedy's wife at a UFC fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bernie Sanders takes Coachella stage to rail against Trump, 'billionaire class' at high-dollar music festival
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders, of Vermont, ripped President Donald Trump and the "billionaire class" while introducing Clairo at the Coachella music festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gretchen Whitmer ribbed online for seeming to hide face in Oval Office
item.description: Democratic Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer temporarily held up a folder to block her face from cameras in an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A retrial of Sarah Palin's case against 'New York Times' begins Monday
item.description: Former Alaska Gov. Sarah Palin has the rare opportunity to retry her defamation case against The New York Times even though she lost it — twice in a 24-hour-period — in early 2022.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: As tariffs put trade between China and the US in peril, Chinese businesses ponder the future
item.description: The 145% tariff from the U.S. and the retaliatory 125% tariff from China are putting businesses doing trade between the U.S. and China on edge.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man deported to El Salvador 'alive and secure': Trump admin
item.description: Lawyers representing Abrego Garcia have proposed the federal government be held in contempt for prolonging efforts to have their client returned.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: White House authorizes military control of land along the border
item.description: In a historic move, the Department of Defense now has control of the Roosevelt Reservation, a 60-foot-wide federal strip.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Maher praises ‘gracious and measured’ Trump after White House visit
item.description: The talk show host emphasized that he did not turn “MAGA” during the meeting over dinner at the White House on Mar. 31.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Tim Scott outlines first 100 days of Congress, crypto, tariffs
item.description: Senate Banking Committee chairman Tim Scott described the first hundred days of Congress, a trade war with China and the timeline for implementing critical crypto policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Longtime House Republican weighs run to flip Dem-held Senate seat in key battleground
item.description: Longtime Republican Rep. Bill Huizenga of Michigan is weighing a run in the 2026 midterm elections for Michigan's open Senate seat, which the GOP is aiming to flip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers reveal whether Americans should pick up the Medicaid tab for illegal immigrants
item.description: Fox News Digital asked lawmakers on Capitol Hill if American taxpayers should have to pick up the tab for Medicaid for illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a fight over proxy voting for new parents upended the US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Anna Paulina Luna was at her Florida home in fall 2023, caring for her newborn son and turning over a question in her mind as a member of Congress: “How do I change this?” Luna, then a first-time mom and first-term lawmaker, could no longer fly to Washington to cast votes in
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump proclaims himself 'in good shape,' but the results of his physical aren't immediately released
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump had an annual physical Friday and concluded, “I did well,” praising his own heart, soul and cognitive ability while noting medical reports from White House doctors may not be ready until the weekend. The 78-year-old, who in January became the oldest in U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: DHS ends protections from deportation for Afghanistan, Cameroon
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has stripped protections from deportation for Afghans and Cameroonians in the U.S., calling into question the ability of some Afghan evacuees to remain in America.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Laredo OKs giving state city land to build border wall
item.description: The City of Laredo is allowing the State of Texas a small section of city-owned land near an international bridge to build a segment of border wall.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump feels in ‘good shape,’ after physical, says he got ‘every question right’ on cognitive test
item.description: President Trump talked to reporters on Friday about his first physical examination and cognitive test of his second term at Walter Reed Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: From classifying immigrants as dead to deportation: A guide to actions on Trump immigration policies
item.description: President Donald Trump's immigration agenda is playing out in numerous ways Friday, from hearings in key cases on the government's power to deport people to the start of a registry required for all those who are in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump opens window for a deal with Iran but issues warning if things don't work out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is betting that a beleaguered Iran is so vulnerable following a tumultuous 18 months in the Middle East that it might finally be ready to abandon its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Picture of Trump after the assassination attempt displaces Obama portrait at the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Friday hung a painting of President Donald Trump depicting a moment after an assassination attempt against him last summer — bumping the official portrait of one of his predecessors, Barack Obama.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadline arrives as Nevada lawmakers work furiously to pass bills out of committee
item.description: A deadline today will be the death knell for some of the nearly 1,100 bills in the Nevada Legislature. Some of the bills never went to a vote. Others were dead on arrival, identified by Gov. Joe Lombardo as bills he would veto.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Bondi appoints new interim U.S. Attorney for San Diego, Imperial counties
item.description: The Southern District of California will have a new U.S attorney after Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed a longtime San Diego prosecutor to take over the role as the region's top federal law enforcement official following Tara McGrath's termination.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Sisolak rematch with Lombardo? Former governor says he's considering it
item.description: Former Gov. Steve Sisolak is considering challenging Gov. Joe Lombardo in 2026, he told Politics Now host John Langeler on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House responds to Supreme Court ruling on mistaken deportation
item.description: The White House on Friday responded to the Supreme Court’s ruling that the Trump administration must “facilitate” the return of a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rather than "effectuate" the return.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP ethics complaint alleges conflict by DFL Senate President Bobby Joe Champion
item.description: Six GOP lawmakers said the Minneapolis Democrat broke Senate rules when he carried legislation funding the nonprofit of a former legal client.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Republicans vying for open Minnesota Senate seat laud conservative values, support for Trump
item.description: The pool of Republican state Senate candidates in north-central Minnesota includes some familiar names in state politics who joined the special election sprint. The GOP primary is April 15.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Now that they've passed a budget plan, the hard part begins for Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans narrowly got their budget plan over the finish line. Now comes the hard part. The resolution adopted this week was only a first step that allows Republicans to draft legislation that they can push through Congress without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Real ID rules take effect in May; Minnesota officials say it’s time to apply
item.description: Starting May 7, travelers will need a Real ID or approved alternative to get through airport security. Officials say about 40 percent of Minnesotans have a Real ID. They’re encouraging everyone to get the necessary travel documents ahead of the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrat introduces proposal to ban book bans
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would ban school districts and staff from banning books without a court order determining the material is obscene.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
